name: Spectral Conquest
description: This algorithm is based on our VST plug-in of the same name. Spectral Conquest is an effect plug-in that lets you directly manipulate the frequency spectrum of audio signals. The incoming signal is analysed via a FFT (Fast Fourier Transform), resulting in the familiar spectrum plot where the full audio spectrum is split into a number of narrow frequency bands. The outgoing audio is then regenerated by an Inverse FFT. The interesting bit is that the plug-in lets you modify the frequency spectrum in between. The algorithm provides a number of ways of manipulating the spectrum from a relatively small number of parameters, including playing the spectrum from a keyboard. The various processes are applied in the order implied by the parameter page ordering (shift, then gate etc.), and may be used at the same time.
gui_description: The display shows the frequency spectrum of the output signal. It also shows the current FFT size, and the module’s total CPU usage (NB not the algorithm’s CPU usage, the total usage).

parameters.name: Latency
parameters.description: Sets the processing latency (which indirectly sets the FFT size).

parameters.name: Sample rate
parameters.description: Sets the sample rate reduction (which indirectly sets the FFT size, and affects the bandwidth).

parameters.name: Dry gain
parameters.description: The output level of the dry signal.

parameters.name: Wet gain
parameters.description: The output level of the wet (processed) signal.

parameters.name: Shift enable
parameters.description: Enables a spectral shift – the spectral energy is shifted by a number of bins up or down.

parameters.name: Shift
parameters.description: The number of bins to shift by.

parameters.name: Reverse
parameters.description: Reverses the spectrum (high frequencies become low frequencies and vice versa).

parameters.name: Gate enable
parameters.description: Enables a spectral gate – each bin is zeroed unless it falls within the gate range.

parameters.name: Upper threshold
parameters.description: Frequency bins with a level above this threshold will be zeroed.

parameters.name: Lower threshold
parameters.description: Frequency bins with a level below this threshold will be zeroed.

parameters.name: Comb enable
parameters.description: Enables a spectral comb filter. ‘Width’ out of every ‘step’ bins are zeroed.

parameters.name: Comb step
parameters.description: Sets the number of bins at which the pattern repeats.

parameters.name: Comb width
parameters.description: Sets the number of bins out of every repeat which are zeroed. The effect will be more pronounced when this is close to ‘step’.

parameters.name: Comb offset
parameters.description: Offsets the start point of the repeating pattern i.e. it sweeps the comb frequencies up & down.

parameters.name: Harmonic enable
parameters.description: Enables a harmonic sieve. Only frequencies which are harmonics of the chosen base frequency will be retained.

parameters.name: Harmonic base
parameters.description: The base frequency for the harmonic sieve.

parameters.name: Harmonic count
parameters.description: The number of harmonics to retain.

parameters.name: Chord enable
parameters.description: Enables a chord sieve. Only frequencies which are in the chosen chord will be retained.

parameters.name: Chord root
parameters.description: The chord root note, as a MIDI note number.

parameters.name: Chord tonic
parameters.description: The level of the chord tonic.

parameters.name: Chord min 2nd
parameters.description: The level of the chord minor 2nd.

parameters.name: Chord maj 2nd
parameters.description: The level of the chord major 2nd.

parameters.name: Chord min 3rd
parameters.description: The level of the chord minor 3rd.

parameters.name: Chord maj 3rd
parameters.description: The level of the chord major 3rd.

parameters.name: Chord 4th
parameters.description: The level of the chord 4th.

parameters.name: Chord tritone
parameters.description: The level of the chord tritone.

parameters.name: Chord 5th
parameters.description: The level of the chord 5th.

parameters.name: Chord min 6th
parameters.description: The level of the chord minor 6th.

parameters.name: Chord maj 6th
parameters.description: The level of the chord major 6th.

parameters.name: Chord min 7th
parameters.description: The level of the chord minor 7th.

parameters.name: Chord maj 7th
parameters.description: The level of the chord major 7th.

parameters.name: Chord octaves
parameters.description: The number of octaves over which to repeat the chord pattern.

parameters.name: Polysynth enable
parameters.description: Enables a sieve based on notes played by CV/gate, MIDI, or I2C. Please refer to the section “Common polysynth features” above.

parameters.name: LP enable
parameters.description: Enables a low-pass filter.

parameters.name: LP cutoff
parameters.description: The low-pass filter cutoff frequency, as a bin number.

parameters.name: HP enable
parameters.description: Enables a high-pass filter.

parameters.name: HP cutoff
parameters.description: The high-pass filter cutoff frequency, as a bin number.

parameters.name: BP enable
parameters.description: Enables a band-pass filter.

parameters.name: BP low
parameters.description: The low pass band frequency of the band-pass filter, as a bin number.

parameters.name: BP high
parameters.description: The high pass band frequency of the band-pass filter, as a bin number.

parameters.name: Notch enable
parameters.description: Enables a notch (band-reject) filter.

parameters.name: Notch low
parameters.description: The low stop band frequency of the notch filter, as a bin number.

parameters.name: Notch high
parameters.description: The high stop band frequency of the notch filter, as a bin number.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above.

input_ports.name: Left/mono input
input_ports.description: The left or mono input audio bus.

input_ports.name: Right input
input_ports.description: The right input audio bus (if the algorithm is set to stereo in the specifications).

input_ports.name: Left/mono input
input_ports.description: The left or mono input audio bus.

input_ports.name: Right input
input_ports.description: The right input audio bus (if the algorithm is set to stereo in the specifications).

output_ports.name: Left/mono output
output_ports.description: The left or mono output audio bus.

output_ports.name: Right output
output_ports.description: The right output audio bus (if the algorithm is set to stereo in the specifications).

output_ports.name: Left/mono output
output_ports.description: The left or mono output audio bus.

output_ports.name: Right output
output_ports.description: The right output audio bus (if the algorithm is set to stereo in the specifications).
